title: Golden Transcripts, net.Pipe, Differential Tests, Fuzzing, Race, and Benchmarks · Language Lens: Netpipe Fragment Schedules
summary: Tests send one complete request through loopback, compare only status, reuse the implementation as its own oracle, fuzz without invariants, and publish benchmark throughput without fragmentation or adversarial peers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the netpipe fragment schedules increment in layered deterministic protocol verification system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use net.Pipe or a scripted short reader and writer to test fragmentation, blocking, deadlines, half-close, cancellation, and cleanup deterministically.
Use exact byte fixtures for valid, incomplete, malformed, normalized, serialized, and residual-buffer cases with readable semantic assertions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Netpipe Fragment Schedules · Theory Netpipe Fragment Schedules
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 9221 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate parser work on transcripts, fragmentation, differential, fuzz, and leak evidence; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
